paper.title: Executable verification through formalized expert reasoning in astronomical spectroscopy

repository.pin_rule: Latest commit returned by the repository commits API whose committer timestamp is not later than the immutable arXiv v1 submission timestamp.
repository.license_observation: The GitHub repository API returned no detected license. The pinned pyproject.toml declares MIT text, while package.json declares ISC; no top-level LICENSE file appears in the pinned recursive tree. Treat redistribution terms as ambiguous pending human review.

scope_notes: The pinned tree contains implementation code and two standard example FITS files plus two counterfactual example traces.

The recursive paper-time tree does not expose the paper's full DESI evaluation catalogue, the 331-case selected cohort, aggregate result tables, or a table-building pipeline under obvious result/catalogue/score paths; reviewers must verify this against code and manuscript rather than infer reproducibility from repository existence.
This acquisition records source availability and lineage only. It is not a code execution, result reproduction, security audit, or deep paper review.
